title: Apply Now: Africa AI Policy Fellowship 2026 for Legal, Policy and Tech Professionals
summary: As Artificial Intelligence continues to reshape industries worldwide, Africa faces a growing need for skilled professionals who can guide its development responsibly. In response, Lawyers Hub has launched the Africa AI Policy Fellowship 2026 —a flagship initiative aimed at nurturing the continent’s next generation of AI governance and regulatory leaders. This prestigious three-month fellowship is designed to bridge the gap between emerging technologies and effective policymaking, ensuring that AI systems across Africa are ethical, inclusive, and contextually relevant. Why the Africa AI Policy Fellowship Matters The rapid evolution of AI technologies has outpaced many existing regulatory frameworks. This fellowship equips participants with the expertise needed to influence policy decisions and build resilient governance systems tailored to African realities. Through the program, fellows will gain hands-on experience in: AI Governance Frameworks – Understanding the legal and regulatory landscape of AI Ethical Impact Analysis – Evaluating the societal and economic implications of AI systems Contextual Policy Design – Developing solutions aligned with Africa’s unique challenges Professional Skill Development – Enhancing research, analytical thinking, and technical writing Who Should Apply? The fellowship targets emerging and mid-career professionals who are passionate about shaping the future of technology policy in Africa. Ideal applicants include: Policy analysts and advisors interested in tech governance Legal professionals and regulatory experts Researchers and academics focused on digital transformation Innovation specialists and development practitioners Key Benefits of the Fellowship Participants will gain access to a robust ecosystem designed to accelerate their careers and impact: Expert Mentorship – Learn from leading African and global AI policy experts Practical Experience – Contribute to real-world policy initiatives Pan-African Networking – Connect with professionals across tech and policy sectors Career Growth Opportunities – Strengthen your profile in governance and emerging technologies How to Apply The application process is straightforward and designed to identify high-potential candidates: Complete the Online Application – A short form that takes approximately 7 minutes Submit a Statement of Interest – Clearly outline your passion for AI policy and career goals Highlight Relevant Experience – Showcase your professional achievements and background APPLY HERE Take the Next Step If you’re ready to play a role in shaping Africa’s digital future, the Africa AI Policy Fellowship 2026 offers a unique platform to build your expertise and influence meaningful change. Visit the official application portal to get started and take the first step toward becoming a leader in AI policy and governance in Africa. title: World Food Programme Internship 2026: Digital Media and Communications Role in Ethiopia
summary: The World Food Programme (WFP) is inviting applications for its Digital Communications Internship based in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. This opportunity is ideal for students and recent graduates in communications, journalism, and related fields who are passionate about humanitarian work and storytelling. WFP is committed to diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunity, encouraging applications from qualified candidates regardless of background. About the World Food Programme (WFP) The World Food Programme (WFP) is the world’s largest humanitarian organization, dedicated to saving lives in emergencies and using food assistance to support long-term recovery, stability, and prosperity. As a 2020 Nobel Peace Prize Laureate , WFP operates globally to assist communities affected by conflict, disasters, and climate change. The organization prioritizes a people-centered approach, building diverse and high-performing teams guided by its core values: Integrity Collaboration Commitment Humanity Inclusion Why Join WFP? Joining WFP offers more than just work experience—it’s a chance to contribute to meaningful global impact. Key benefits include: A diverse and inclusive multicultural work environment Access to training, mentorship, and professional development programs Opportunities for career growth and internal mobility Collaboration with passionate professionals across global offices Competitive compensation package Internship Overview Position: Intern (Digital Communications) Location: Addis Ababa, Ethiopia Duration: 8 Months Eligibility: Open to Ethiopian nationals Deadline: 30th March 2026 Role Purpose Under the supervision of the communications team, the intern will support the creation of innovative, high-quality multimedia content targeted at key audiences such as donors, partners, government stakeholders, and beneficiaries. Key Responsibilities As a Digital Communications Intern, you will: Develop communication materials including social media content, stories, and visuals Collect field content such as photos, testimonials, and quotes Draft content for digital platforms under supervision Ensure consistency in branding and messaging Coordinate with designers, vendors, and printers Support social media scheduling, monitoring, and reporting Provide general administrative support for digital communications Perform additional communication-related tasks as assigned Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet one of the following conditions : Currently enrolled in an undergraduate program (with at least 2 years completed) Currently enrolled in a graduate program Recent graduate (within 6 months) in communications, journalism, or a related field Additional requirements: Fluency in English and Amharic (written and spoken) Ability to work in Addis Ababa Must not be an immediate family member of a WFP employee Required Skills and Competencies Ideal candidates should have: Proficiency in tools such as Adobe Premiere Pro, InDesign, Illustrator, Photoshop, After Effects, Lightroom, Canva , and MS Office Strong digital storytelling and content creation skills Understanding of communication strategies and humanitarian contexts A creative portfolio demonstrating attention to detail Ability to work independently and in diverse teams Flexibility and adaptability in a dynamic work environment WFP Leadership Framework WFP operates under a leadership framework that defines expected behaviors and standards for collaboration and impact. Candidates are encouraged to familiarize themselves with these principles before applying. Diversity, Inclusion Equal Opportunity WFP is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an inclusive workplace. Applications are encouraged from individuals of all backgrounds, including persons with disabilities. Reasonable accommodations are available throughout the recruitment process. Important Application Information Before applying, take note of the following: Applications must be submitted through WFP’s official online recruitment system Required documents: CV and Cover Letter Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted WFP does not charge any fees at any stage of the recruitment process Application Tips Ensure your profile is complete and up to date (education, experience, skills) Tailor your CV and cover letter to highlight digital communication skills and creativity Include a portfolio showcasing your work if available APPLY HERE Start Your Humanitarian Career with WFP This internship is a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in digital communications while contributing to life-saving humanitarian efforts. If you are passionate about storytelling, media, and global impact, this could be your pathway into the international development sector. Discover more similar opportunities here. title: Apply Now: ACNEI 2026 Free Online Course in Computational Neuroscience for African Students
summary: The African Computational Neuroscience Educational Initiative (ACNEI) is inviting applications for its 10-Day Introductory Online Training Program under the theme: “Foundations of Computational Neuroscience for Young African Scholars.” This unique program is designed to inspire and equip the next generation of African thinkers with foundational skills in neuroscience, computation, and data-driven problem solving. Computational neuroscience is a rapidly growing interdisciplinary field that blends brain science, artificial intelligence, data science, and medicine . However, access to structured learning opportunities in this field remains limited across much of Africa. ACNEI aims to bridge this gap by democratizing access to high-quality training and preparing students for future careers in neuroscience and AI-related disciplines. Program Overview Program Dates: 11th – 22nd May 2026 Optional Regional Hub Training: 25th – 28th May 2026 Format: Fully Online Duration: 2 weeks (2 hours per day) Target Audience: Post-high school students and early undergraduate students across Africa Cost: Free (selection-based) What You Will Learn Participants in the ACNEI Introductory School will gain: A foundational understanding of how the brain works and why computation is essential Beginner-friendly Python programming skills tailored for neuroscience applications Insights into neurons and neural networks as computational systems Hands-on experience through mini-projects (no prior coding experience required) Exposure to African-centered neuroscience and AI case studies Mentorship and career guidance from global experts in the field Why You Should Apply This program is more than just an introduction—it is a gateway into one of the most exciting and impactful fields shaping the future of science and technology. Whether you aspire to work in AI, healthcare, research, or data science , this training provides the foundational knowledge, mentorship, and network needed to get started. If you are a young African student curious about how the brain works and how technology can help us understand it, this is your opportunity to begin that journey. Who Should Apply? ACNEI strongly encourages applications from: African post-high school students, gap-year students, and early undergraduates Students interested in biology, medicine, engineering, computer science, psychology, artificial intelligence, neuroscience, or data science Applicants with little or no prior coding experience Individuals from underrepresented regions and backgrounds across Africa Benefits of the Program Participants selected for the program will receive: A Certificate of Completion Access to the ACNEI mentorship and alumni network Priority consideration for advanced ACNEI programs and regional training hubs Exposure to global open-science and neuroscience communities Application Details Application Deadline: March 29th, 2026 Program Start Date: May 11th, 2026 Interested applicants are encouraged to apply before the deadline to secure their spot in this competitive and impactful program. Apply Here Discover similar opportunities here. The post Apply Now: ACNEI 2026 Free Online Course in Computational Neuroscience for African Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Youth Policy Development Group 2026: Empower the Next Generation of UK Policy Leaders
summary: Are you a young person in the UK ready to make your voice heard where it matters most? The Youth Policy Development Group (YPDG) 2026 , relaunched by Shout Out UK, is your gateway to influencing national conversations, shaping policy, and building real-world skills in governance and advocacy. This is more than just a program—it’s a platform for young leaders to contribute directly to decision-making processes and represent the views of their generation. What is the Youth Policy Development Group (YPDG)? The Youth Policy Development Group (YPDG) is a dynamic initiative designed to bring together passionate individuals aged 14–25 from across the UK. Participants collaborate on policy research, engage in meaningful discussions, and present their findings directly to policymakers and government officials. Through this program, you will: Conduct in-depth policy research on key national issues Collaborate with a diverse group of young thinkers Present policy recommendations to decision-makers Participate in roundtable discussions and live events Why You Should Apply Joining YPDG offers a range of valuable benefits that can shape your future: 1. Direct Impact on Policy Have your ideas heard by politicians and government departments, ensuring youth perspectives are included in national decisions. 2. Skill Development Enhance your knowledge in political literacy, media awareness, research, and public speaking. 3. Professional Training Mentorship Receive structured online training sessions and one-on-one support from experienced professionals at Shout Out UK. 4. Networking Opportunities Connect with policymakers, activists, and like-minded young leaders across the UK. 5. Personal Growth Build confidence, leadership skills, and a strong understanding of civic engagement. What to Expect from the Programme As a YPDG participant, you will: Work on policy briefs addressing real-world issues Attend training sessions to strengthen your analytical and communication skills Receive personalized feedback and mentorship Engage in in-person and virtual discussions Represent youth voices in high-level conversations Eligibility Criteria To apply, you must: Be aged 14–25 years Currently reside in the United Kingdom (England, Scotland, Wales, or Northern Ireland) Have a passion for policy, research, and social impact Application Deadline Make sure to submit your application before the deadline: Deadline: Sunday, 29th March 2026 Late applications will not be considered, so apply early to secure your spot. How to Apply Interested candidates can express their interest by completing the official application form: APPLY HERE. For inquiries: contact@shoutoutuk.org For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Final Thoughts If you’re passionate about shaping the future and want to contribute meaningfully to national discussions, the Youth Policy Development Group 2026 is the perfect opportunity. Step forward, share your ideas, and become part of a movement that empowers young voices across the UK. title: Visiting Researchers Program 2026/2027 at the Max Planck Institute for the Study of Societies (MPIfG)
summary: The Max Planck Institute for the Study of Societies (MPIfG) is now accepting applications for its prestigious Visiting Researchers Program for the winter semester 2026/2027. This is an excellent opportunity for scholars in economic sociology and political economy to engage in high-level research within one of the world’s leading institutes for social science research. 🧠 About the Program The MPIfG conducts cutting-edge research on the governance of modern societies, focusing on the intersection of social, economic, and political systems. The Visiting Researchers Program is designed to foster collaboration and intellectual exchange among global scholars working in: Economic Sociology Comparative Political Economy International Political Economy Participants will be integrated into a dynamic research environment and collaborate with leading experts in the field. ⏳ Duration of Stay Selected visiting researchers can stay at MPIfG for a period ranging from 2 to 12 months during the winter semester 2026/2027. 👩‍🏫 Who Can Apply? Applications are welcome from: Professors Postdoctoral researchers Doctoral researchers Candidates from all countries are encouraged to apply, provided their research aligns with MPIfG’s core themes and ongoing projects. 📄 Application Requirements Applicants must submit: A research proposal (maximum 3 pages) outlining their intended project A funding plan (as candidates are required to secure their own funding) An indication of a preferred MPIfG director or research group leader Successful applicants will receive an official invitation letter to support their external funding applications. 🏆 Selection Criteria Candidates will be selected based on: Academic excellence Relevance and quality of the research proposal Alignment with MPIfG’s research agenda 🌐 Research Environment Benefits Access to a collaborative, interdisciplinary academic community Engagement with leading scholars in sociology and political science Opportunities to contribute to ongoing research discussions and projects Supportive and inclusive institutional culture promoting equality and diversity ⚠️ Important Notes Visiting researchers must arrange their own funding Accepted stay periods cannot be postponed or split Applications must be submitted online only (email submissions are not accepted) 📅 Application Deadline The deadline to apply is April 30, 2026 (23:59 UTC+2) . Late submissions will not be considered. 🔗 How to Apply Interested candidates should visit the official MPIfG website to access the application portal and detailed guidelines. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . title: Powerful NASA Pathways Internship Virtual Event 2026 to Launch Your Career
summary: The Powerful NASA Pathways Internship Virtual Event 2026 is an exciting opportunity for students interested in launching their careers with one of the world’s most prestigious space and research organizations. This virtual information session provides direct access to NASA representatives and offers valuable insights into one of NASA’s most competitive early-career pathways. Designed to introduce students to the NASA Pathways Internship Program, this event is an essential first step for those aiming to gain hands-on experience in science, engineering, and other professional fields while contributing to real NASA missions. The event will take place on March 26, 2026, with registration closing on March 25, 2026. About the NASA Pathways Internship Program The NASA Pathways Internship Program is a highly sought-after initiative that provides students with multi-semester, hands-on experience working alongside NASA engineers, scientists, and professionals. This program is more than just an internship—it serves as a direct pipeline to full-time employment at NASA after graduation. Interns are given the opportunity to contribute to real-world projects that support NASA’s mission, gaining practical skills and industry exposure that are unmatched in the STEM field. Through this program, participants not only build technical expertise but also develop critical thinking, teamwork, and problem-solving skills that are essential for careers in space exploration and beyond. What to Expect from the Virtual Event The Powerful NASA Pathways Internship Virtual Event 2026 is designed to provide a comprehensive overview of internship opportunities at NASA and guide participants through the application process. During this interactive session, attendees will: • Learn about NASA internship opportunities and career pathways • Understand eligibility requirements and application processes • Receive tips on building a strong and competitive resume • Gain insights into interview preparation and online assessments • Engage directly with NASA Pathways and OSTEM representatives The session will be hosted on Microsoft Teams, and registered participants will receive the access link via email before the event. Event Details Event Date: March 26, 2026 Time: 3:30 PM (EDT) Registration Deadline: March 25, 2026 Platform: Microsoft Teams This virtual format allows students from across the United States to participate and learn about opportunities without the need for travel. Why This Opportunity Matters The NASA Pathways Internship Program is one of the most competitive and rewarding early-career opportunities available to students in STEM and related fields. By attending this virtual event, participants gain insider knowledge that can significantly improve their chances of securing an internship with NASA. Understanding how to navigate the application process, prepare for interviews, and present a strong resume can make a major difference in standing out as a candidate. Additionally, the program provides a clear pathway to long-term career opportunities within NASA, making it an ideal choice for students who are serious about pursuing a future in science, engineering, or public service. Who Should Attend This event is specifically designed for students who are interested in exploring career opportunities at NASA and gaining experience through internships. It is open to: • College sophomores and juniors • Seniors planning to attend graduate school • Current graduate students Participants should have an interest in STEM careers and be motivated to contribute to NASA’s mission of exploration, innovation, and discovery. How to Register Interested participants must register online before the deadline to secure their spot in the event. Once registered, attendees will receive further instructions and access details via email. It is recommended to register early to ensure participation and allow time to prepare for the session. Deadline for Registration The registration deadline for the Powerful NASA Pathways Internship Virtual Event 2026 is March 25, 2026. The event itself will take place on March 26, 2026. Learn More About This Opportunity To learn more about the Powerful NASA Pathways Internship Virtual Event 2026 and register, click here : Conclusion The NASA Pathways Internship Virtual Event 2026 is a powerful opportunity for students to take the first step toward a career with NASA. By attending this session, participants gain valuable knowledge, connect with professionals, and position themselves for future internship and employment opportunities within one of the most respected organizations in the world. title: Canada Fund for Local Initiatives (CFLI) 2026: Funding Opportunity for Local Organizations in Côte d’Ivoire and Liberia
summary: The Canada Fund for Local Initiatives (CFLI), an initiative by Global Affairs Canada, is now accepting applications for its 2026 funding cycle. This program is designed to support small, high-impact projects led by local organizations in developing countries, including Côte d’Ivoire and Liberia. With an annual budget of CAD $26.8 million, CFLI empowers grassroots organizations to implement innovative solutions that create measurable and lasting change within their communities. About the CFLI Program The CFLI funds approximately 650 projects annually across more than 120 eligible countries. What makes this initiative unique is its strong focus on local leadership—projects are designed and implemented by local organizations that understand community needs best. These projects are selected and approved by Canadian diplomatic missions, including the Canadian Embassy responsible for Côte d’Ivoire and Liberia. Funding Details Funding Amount: Typically between CAD $20,000 and $49,000 Average Grant Size: Around CAD $31,000 Project Duration: April 1, 2026 – March 31, 2027 Projects extending beyond this period may still be considered, but any additional funding will only be disbursed after April 1, 2027. Key Focus Areas CFLI prioritizes projects that align with Canada’s international development goals and produce tangible community impact. These include: Inclusive governance, democracy, and human rights Gender equality and empowerment of women and girls Peacebuilding and conflict prevention Economic growth that benefits vulnerable populations Environmental sustainability and climate action Health, education, and nutrition (human dignity) Emergency and disaster response initiatives Who Can Apply? Eligible applicants include: Local non-governmental and community-based organizations Local academic institutions implementing community-based projects Municipal, regional, or national public institutions While priority is given to local civil society organizations, international NGOs and other entities may apply if they partner with local organizations. Why Apply? The CFLI is especially valuable for smaller organizations that may not qualify for large-scale funding. It offers a unique opportunity to: Scale impactful community projects Access international funding support Strengthen local development initiatives Build long-term sustainability and resilience Application Deadline Côte d’Ivoire: March 27, 2026 (23:59 GMT) Liberia: March 27, 2026 (23:59 GMT) Applicants are encouraged to submit their proposals well before the deadline to ensure consideration. How to Apply To learn more about the application process and submit your proposal: APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more grant opportunities, click here . Final Thoughts If you are part of a local organization working to improve lives and strengthen communities, this is a powerful opportunity to access funding and amplify your impact. The CFLI continues to champion grassroots innovation and sustainable development—don’t miss your chance to be part of it. title: Apply for the Wilmers Integrity Prize — Win One of Three $100,000 Awards
summary: Are you leading meaningful change in your community but haven’t yet received the recognition or funding you deserve? The Robert G. Wilmers Integrity Prize 2026 is now open, offering three unrestricted $100,000 awards to individuals making a real impact across the United States. This prestigious prize is designed to support individual changemakers — not organizations — who are deeply committed to advancing the greater good. Whether your work focuses on social justice, education, healthcare, or environmental change, this opportunity is meant to elevate leaders who are already making progress and have the potential to scale their impact even further. About the Prize The Wilmers Integrity Prize honors individuals who demonstrate a rare combination of impact, integrity, and leadership . Unlike many awards, this prize specifically targets those who are still building momentum — not those who are already well-funded or widely recognized. Each year, three winners receive $100,000 each , with funding that is completely unrestricted. This means recipients can use the award in whatever way best supports their work, whether that’s expanding their initiatives, investing in resources, or sustaining themselves as they continue their efforts. The prize supports work across a wide range of fields, including: Arts and culture Education Environment Law and justice Medicine and healthcare Nutrition Social justice and social reform Why This Opportunity Matters Many individuals doing impactful work operate without the recognition or funding they need to scale their efforts. The Wilmers Integrity Prize fills this gap by investing directly in people who are deeply committed to change but are still building toward larger impact. By focusing on integrity as much as outcomes, the prize highlights the importance of ethical leadership in creating sustainable, meaningful change. For applicants, this is more than just funding — it is an opportunity to gain recognition, expand your reach, and accelerate your work in a significant way. What You’ll Gain One of the most powerful aspects of this opportunity is the scale of funding . Receiving $100,000 in unrestricted support can be truly transformative — especially for individuals who have not yet had access to major funding streams. Winners also gain national recognition , which can significantly increase visibility for their work. This can open doors to new partnerships, collaborations, and additional funding opportunities. Another key benefit is validation of impact and integrity . The prize is not just about what you’ve achieved, but how you’ve achieved it — recognizing leaders who act with courage, independence, and strong ethical values. Additionally, finalists and winners go through a rigorous selection process, including site visits and evaluations, which can further strengthen their work and positioning. Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to individuals who meet the following criteria: Must be based in and primarily working in the United States Must be an individual (not an organization) Must be actively working to advance the greater good Must demonstrate strong integrity, leadership, and independence Must show measurable progress and potential for growth Importantly, this prize is intended for individuals for whom $100,000 would be a true game-changer — not those who already have significant access to funding or established philanthropic support. Applicants can either apply directly or be nominated by someone else. Selection Criteria Applications are evaluated based on three core pillars: Cause Your work must address real challenges and contribute to positive change within a community at the local or national level. Character Applicants should demonstrate qualities such as integrity, courage, curiosity, independence, and a commitment to their principles. Results Your efforts should already show meaningful progress, with strong potential for continued and expanded impact. The selection process includes application screening, evaluation by a selection committee, and further vetting of finalists, including reference checks and site visits. Application Process and Deadline Applicants must complete the online application form or be nominated through the official platform. The process is designed to be straightforward, with no application fee. Application Deadline: March 31, 2026 Finalists will be contacted for additional steps, and winners will be announced at an award ceremony in October in Buffalo, New York. Learn more and apply through the official website. Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. The post Apply for the Wilmers Integrity Prize — Win One of Three $100,000 Awards appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for MiMentor Health Equity Fellowship 2026 in California
summary: Are you an international-minded aspiring healthcare professional committed to addressing health disparities? The MiMentor Health Equity Fellowship 2026 offers a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience, mentorship, and financial support while working directly with underserved communities across California. This eight-month, part-time fellowship is designed for post-undergraduate individuals who are preparing for careers in healthcare, including medicine, behavioral health, and public health. Through a combination of clinical exposure, community engagement, and professional development, the program equips fellows with the tools needed to become leaders in health equity. About the Fellowship The MiMentor Health Equity Fellowship is a structured program running over eight months beginning in Fall 2026. Fellows are placed at partner healthcare and community-based organizations across California, where they contribute to projects focused on improving health outcomes in underserved populations. Participants commit approximately 20 hours per week , totaling around 640 hours throughout the program. The fellowship combines practical field experience with virtual learning sessions, allowing fellows to balance hands-on work with skill development. Placement sites include organizations such as healthcare networks, universities, and community initiatives in locations like Fresno, Los Angeles, San Diego, and Riverside. Each site offers a unique focus, ranging from clinical outreach and public health programming to data-driven health improvement projects. Why This Opportunity Matters Health disparities continue to affect communities across the United States, particularly among underserved populations. Addressing these challenges requires a new generation of healthcare professionals who are not only clinically skilled but also deeply committed to equity and inclusion. The MiMentor Health Equity Fellowship plays a crucial role in preparing future leaders by combining education, mentorship, and real-world experience. It helps bridge the gap between academic learning and practical application, ensuring that fellows are equipped to make meaningful contributions to healthcare systems. For participants, this fellowship is more than just a training program — it is a stepping stone toward impactful careers in medicine, public health, and social justice. What Participants Will Gain One of the most significant benefits of this fellowship is the financial support provided to participants . Selected fellows receive a $13,000 stipend , disbursed biweekly, which helps make participation more accessible. Beyond financial support, fellows gain extensive hands-on experience in healthcare settings . This includes clinical shadowing, working alongside multidisciplinary teams, and participating in real-world health equity initiatives. The program also offers one-to-one mentorship with experienced professionals. This personalized guidance supports fellows in navigating their academic and career pathways while building confidence and professional skills. Participants will benefit from professional development resources , including application support for medical and health-related programs, academic advising, and career coaching. Another key component is the health improvement project , where fellows design and implement a project addressing community health challenges. This experience strengthens research, analytical, and project management skills. Additionally, fellows participate in virtual workshops covering topics such as health disparities, policy, community outreach, research methods, and mental health. These sessions provide both theoretical knowledge and practical tools. The fellowship also offers opportunities for networking and visibility , including potential participation in conferences and presentations of project outcomes. Who Can Apply The fellowship is open to individuals who meet the following requirements: Must have completed an undergraduate degree Must be a California resident at the time of application Must be interested in pursuing a career in healthcare or behavioral health Must have or create a complete MiMentor profile Must be able to complete onboarding requirements, including health screenings and background checks While the program is open to all eligible applicants, preference is given to individuals who are: First-generation students From historically underrepresented or marginalized communities Committed to working in underserved populations Applicants must also demonstrate a strong commitment to community engagement and health equity. Fellowship Experience Fellows will engage in a wide range of activities, including: Clinical shadowing and exposure to healthcare teams Community outreach and engagement projects Research and data analysis Participation in health equity initiatives Collaboration with medical students and professionals They will also complete a community-based project and present their findings, contributing to real-world solutions in healthcare. Application Process and Deadline Applicants must submit a complete application, including: Resume Unofficial transcripts Completed application form Two references Shortlisted candidates will be invited for virtual interviews, and selected applicants will undergo a background check. Application Deadline: 29 March 2026 Decisions Released: April 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure all materials are submitted correctly. Learn more and apply through the official MiMentor platform. Learn more in the official call for applications. Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Abel Prize Travel Fellowships 2026 for Science Journalists in Oslo, Norway (Fully Funded Opportunity)
summary: Science journalists worldwide now have an exceptional opportunity to gain global exposure and engage with leading mathematicians through the Abel Prize Travel Fellowships 2026. Offered by the World Federation of Science Journalists (WFSJ) in collaboration with the Norwegian Academy of Science and Letters, this prestigious fellowship enables selected journalists to attend the renowned Abel Prize Week in Oslo, Norway. Scheduled to take place from 25–28 May 2026, the Abel Prize Week celebrates groundbreaking achievements in mathematics and offers unparalleled access to the global scientific community. About the Abel Prize Travel Fellowships The Abel Prize is one of the world’s most prestigious awards in mathematics. Through this fellowship, two science journalists will be selected to cover the event, interact with top mathematicians, and report on cutting-edge research recognized by the prize. Participants will gain exclusive access to official events, including the Abel Prize award ceremony, and will have the opportunity to interview the laureate(s). Two fellowships will be awarded: One for a science journalist from anywhere in the world One for a science journalist based in Europe What the Fellowship Covers The fellowship provides comprehensive support to ensure a seamless experience for selected participants. Benefits include: Round-trip travel costs to Oslo (arranged and reimbursed by WFSJ) Travel health insurance Per diem for ground transportation to and from airports Hotel accommodation (including breakfast) during the program Access to all official Abel Prize Week events Participation in exclusive activities organized by the Norwegian Academy Opportunities to interview Abel Prize laureates Visa support documentation, if required Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be a professional science journalist working full-time (staff or freelance) Have recent experience (within the last five years) in science journalism Preferably demonstrate coverage related to mathematics or fundamental science Be able to attend the full Abel Prize Week from 25–28 May 2026 Work across print, digital, broadcast, podcasting, or multimedia platforms Application Requirements Applicants must submit the following documents: Curriculum Vitae including contact details, professional affiliation, and social media handles (if applicable) A one-page essay (maximum 500 words) in English explaining motivation and intended journalistic work Three samples of published or broadcast work (links to articles, audio, or video) English summaries or translations for non-English work Copy of passport identification page Applications must be sent via email to: info@wfsj.org Subject line: Application: Abel Prize Travel Fellowships for Science Journalists Selection Process Applications will be reviewed by a jury composed of members of the WFSJ Board or nominees appointed by the Board. Two candidates will be selected based on professional experience, quality of work, and potential impact. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. Key Dates Application Deadline: 5 April 2026 Notification of Selected Fellows: 18 April 2026 Abel Prize Week: 25–28 May 2026 Additional Information For further inquiries, contact: Subhra Priyadarshini (WFSJ Board Director) Email: subhra.priyadarshini@wfsj.org The post Abel Prize Travel Fellowships 2026 for Science Journalists in Oslo, Norway (Fully Funded Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
